name: What are the most common mistakes people make when installing a door themselves?
headline: What are the most common mistakes people make when installing a door themselves?

mainEntity.name: What are the most common mistakes people make when installing a door themselves?
mainEntity.text: What are the most common mistakes people make when installing a door themselves?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common mistakes during DIY door installation mostly come down to measuring and alignment. One big error is not measuring the rough opening correctly and buying a door that’s too big or too small. Even a small size mismatch can lead to gaps, binding, or expensive adjustments. Another frequent mistake is failing to make the frame truly level and plumb before securing it. People sometimes rely on the existing framing, assuming it’s straight, and then discover the new door swings open by itself or rubs at the top or bottom. Proper shimming along the hinges and latch side is essential. Many DIYers also rush the weatherproofing on exterior doors. Skipping proper flashing, caulk, or foam insulation can allow water and air leaks. Overfilling the gap with expanding foam can bend the frame and cause sticking. Pitfalls to watch out for: tightening screws fully before the frame is perfectly adjusted, ignoring the manufacturer’s instructions, and cutting into stucco or siding without a plan to repair and seal it. Removing too much material around the opening can weaken the wall. To avoid these issues, take time to double-check measurements, use a quality level, and adjust the frame before final fastening. If you hit structural surprises like rot, cracked studs, or major gaps, consider pausing and getting professional help so the door remains safe, secure, and weather-tight.
